arts.ttl: Daily Schmankerl: Bayern Munich, Joshua Kimmich heading toward a new deal?; Bayern eyeing Manchester United’s Marcus Rashford?; FC Barcelona to target Liverpool’s Darwin Nunez if it misses on Man City’s Erling Haaland; and MORE!
arts.sum: After rumors linking him to Paris Saint-Germain, Manchester City, Liverpool FC, Arsenal FC, Real Madrid, and FC Barcelona have been swirling for months, it appears that Joshua Kimmich and Bayern Munich could be lining up for a contract extension, which should satisfy both parties. Could Bayern Munich be thinking about a pursuit of Manchester United attacker Marcus Rashford?

arts.ttl: Olmo explains how Raphinha is inspiring Barca
arts.sum: Dani Olmo has been full of praise for Raphinha after seeing the Brazilian make a brilliant start to the season with Barcelona . Yet Olmo says it’s Raphinha’s work rate and desire that is inspiring his team-mates under Hansi Flick.

arts.ttl: Bavarian Loan Works: Alexander Nübel comes up short against Bayern Munich
arts.sum: There are five players on loan in the German Bundesliga: Alexander Nübel, Franz Krätzig, Paul Wanner, and Armando Sieb. Bayern Munich played against two of their loanees this week — Alexander Nübel on the men’s team and Karólína Lea Vilhjálmsdóttir.

arts.ttl: FC Bayern Campus Round Up: Luca Denk continues his streak of long shots, Ibrahimović continues his road to recovery, birth of a goalscoring right-back and more...
arts.sum: Individually, Luca Denk impressed with his third long range goal in 3 games with a gorgeously curved free kick into the top corner, Nestory Irankunda worked hard all night and was rewarded with an assist for Arijon Ibrahimović and the substitutes in Guido Della Rovere, Timo Kern and Noel Aseko Nkili helped take the team performance to the next level. In fact, if one watches the highlights, the goals came from a direct free kick, a counter attack from an opposition corner and a corner of the team’s own.

arts.ttl: Kompany goes crazy for Cubarsi ahead of Bayern vs Barca
arts.sum: “I don’t know, honestly, it’s exceptional for Barcelona that, after such a short time since the departure of Messi, who for many years was a key figure in world football, they already have another player who can replace him. Bayern Munich boss Vincent Kompany has been full of praise for Pau Cubarsi ahead of Wednesday’s Champions League tie between the two sides.

arts.ttl: FC Barcelona’s Raphinha not concerned with revenge vs. Bayern Munich in the Champions League
arts.sum: FC Barcelona attacker Raphinha knows the recent history between his club and Bayern Munich, but does not want to make Wednesday’s Champions League match to be about retribution. It’s a pity what happened in the last few games against Bayern, but we are focused on this game.

arts.ttl: Bayern Munich’s Joshua Kimmich reminisces about FC Barcelona’s Hansi Flick, ready for Champions League showdown
arts.sum: We players see the path and believe in the way we’re playing,” said Kimmich, who then reflected on his time working with Flick at Bayern Munich. “It feels like the time under Hansi Flick because we’re winning so many games.

arts.ttl: Barcelona transfer news: Bayern Munich's Joshua Kimmich addresses Camp Nou links
arts.sum: Joshua Kimmich leaves the door open to an exit from Bayern Munich amid suggestions that Barcelona are keen to sign him on a free transfer.

arts.ttl: Barcelona boss Flick still has one doubt for starting XI vs Bayern Munich
arts.sum: Barcelona head coach Hansi Flick is yet to decide on one key position in his starting XI to face Bayern Munich on Wednesday. That’s according to Barca insider Javier Miguel…

arts.ttl: Papers: Barca legend Xavi in frame if Man Utd axe Ten Hag
arts.sum: The top stories and transfer rumours from Wednesday's newspapers...
